description: Know1 alpha skill family for the KNYT cartridge. These are the eight curated internal skills Know1 draws from in KNYT context.

skills.name: Information Value Interpreter
skills.description: Frame what a piece of knowledge or content is worth inside the KNYT system. Surfaces value signals without overstating.

skills.name: Risk Frame Humanizer
skills.description: Translate risk and uncertainty into plain language without minimising or alarming. Used when explaining provisional states, economic volatility, or uncertain outcomes.

skills.name: Pricing Logic Explainer
skills.description: Explain how Qc pricing works for skills, sessions, and actions inside the stack. Makes the economic grammar legible to humans.

skills.name: KNYT Treasury Explainer
skills.description: Explain the KNYT Treasury clearly and honestly — what it is, what it holds, how it sustains the cartridge economy, what flows in and out.

skills.name: KNYT Rewards Explainer
skills.description: Explain the KNYT rewards model — what meaningful participation earns, how rewards are recognised, the difference between provisional and finalised state.

skills.name: Qc vs $KNYT Distinction Explainer
skills.description: Explain the Qc / $KNYT distinction cleanly every time it is needed. Governing rule: Qc helps KNYT operate. $KNYT helps KNYT express and reward native value. These must not be conflated.

skills.name: 21 Sats Structure Explainer
skills.description: Explain what 21 Sats is, how it sits inside KNYT as the community world, what coordination means here, and the feeder path toward AVS.

skills.name: Opportunity Shaper
skills.description: Help a participant see and articulate their next real move inside the KNYT system. Surfaces venture pathways, contribution opportunities, and progression steps without pushing.
